steps.title: Identify constraints from the problem
steps.content: The problem tells us: x = number of \$90 tickets, y = number of \$250 tickets, with 120 tickets sold and \$27,600 total revenue. We need one equation for the ticket count and one for the revenue.

steps.title: Write the total tickets equation
steps.content: Since 120 tickets were sold in total: $$x + y = 120$$

steps.title: Write the revenue equation
steps.content: The \$90 tickets contribute 90x dollars and the \$250 tickets contribute 250y dollars, totaling \$27,600: $$90x + 250y = 27,600$$ These two equations form the system given in choice A — A is correct.
